texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: Windows XP IRQ conflicts occur if a device either is not detected during the operating system setup or is detected but incorrectly configured.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: To check for conflicts on a computer running Windows XP:

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button and click Control Panel .

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 2 Click Performance and Maintenance and click System .

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 3 Click the Hardware tab and click Device Manager .

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 4 In the Device Manager list, check for conflicts with the other devices.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: Conflicts are indicated by a yellow exclamation point ( ! ) beside the conflicting device or a red X if the device has been disabled.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 5 Double-click any conflict to display the Properties window.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: If an IRQ conflict exists, the Device status area in the Properties window reports the cards or devices that share the device's IRQ.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 6 Resolve conflicts by reconfiguring the devices or removing the devices from the Device Manager .

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: To use the Windows XP Hardware Troubleshooter:

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button and click Help and Support .

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 2 Type hardware troubleshooter in the Search field and click the arrow to start the search.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 3 Click Hardware Troubleshooter in the Search Results list.

texts.section_name: Resolving Software and Hardware Incompatibilities
texts.content: 4 In the Hardware Troubleshooter list, click I need to resolve a hardware conflict on my computer , and click Next .

texts.section_name: Using System Restore
texts.content: Using System Restore

texts.section_name: Using System Restore
texts.content: The Microsoft ® Windows ® XP operating system provides System Restore to allow you to return your computer to an earlier operating state (without affecting data files) if changes to the hardware, software, or other system settings have left the computer in an undesirable operating state. See the Help and Support Center for information on using System Restore. To access help, see page 80.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not monitor your data files or recover them.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: Creating a Restore Point

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button and click Help and Support .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 2 Click System Restore .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 3 Follow the instructions on the screen.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: Restoring the Computer to an Earlier Operating State

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: If problems occur after you install a device driver, use Device Driver Rollback (see page 89) to resolve the problem. If that is unsuccessful, then use System Restore.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: NOTICE: Before you restore the computer to an earlier operating state, save and close all open files and close all open programs. Do not alter, open, or delete any files or programs until the system restoration is complete.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button, point to All Programs → Accessories → System Tools , and then click System Restore .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 2 Ensure that Restore my computer to an earlier time is selected and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 3 Click a calendar date to which you want to restore your computer.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: The Select a Restore Point screen provides a calendar that allows you to see and select restore points. All calendar dates with available restore points appear in bold.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 4 Select a restore point and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: If a calendar date has only one restore point, then that restore point is automatically selected. If two or more restore points are available, click the restore point that you prefer.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: The Restoration Complete screen appears after System Restore finishes collecting data and then the computer restarts.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 6 After the computer restarts, click OK .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: To change the restore point, you can either repeat the steps using a different restore point, or you can undo the restoration.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: Undoing the Last System Restore

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: NOTICE: Before you undo the last system restore, save and close all open files and close all open programs. Do not alter, open, or delete any files or programs until the system restoration is complete.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button, point to All Programs → Accessories → System Tools , and then click System Restore .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 2 Click Undo my last restoration and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: The System Restore screen appears and the computer restarts.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 4 After the computer restarts, click OK .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: Enabling System Restore

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: If you reinstall Windows XP with less than 200 MB of free hard-disk space available, System Restore is automatically disabled. To see if System Restore is enabled:

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button and click Control Panel .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 2 Click Performance and Maintenance .

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 4 Click the System Restore tab.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Make regular backups of your data files. System Restore does not...
texts.content: 5 Ensure that Turn off System Restore is unchecked.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: Before You Reinstall

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: If you are reinstalling the Windows XP operating system to correct a problem with a newly installed driver, use Windows XP Device Driver Rollback (see page 89) to replace the device driver with the previously installed version.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: If Device Driver Rollback does not resolve the problem, then use System Restore (see page 92) to return your operating system to the operating state it was in before you installed the new device driver.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: Reinstalling Windows XP

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: To reinstall Windows XP, perform the steps in the following sections in the order in which they are listed.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: The reinstallation process can take 1 to 2 hours to complete. After the reinstallation is complete, you need to reinstall the device drivers, virus protection program, and other software.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: NOTICE: The Operating System CD provides options for reinstalling Windows XP. The options can overwrite files and possibly affect programs installed on your hard drive. Therefore, do not reinstall Windows XP unless instructed to do so by a Dell technical support representative.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: NOTICE: To prevent conflicts with Windows XP, disable any virus protection software installed on your computer before you reinstall Windows XP. See the documentation that came with the software for instructions.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: Preparing to Boot From the Operating System CD

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: 1 Insert the Operating System CD.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: 2 Exit any program or utility that might run after you insert the CD.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: 3 Shut down (see page 40) and restart the computer.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: 4 Press immediately after the DELL™ logo appears.

texts.section_name: Reinstalling Microsoft ® Windows ® XP
texts.content: If the operating system logo appears, wait until you see the Windows desktop, and then shut down the computer and try again.

texts.section_name: 5 Use the arrow keys to select CD-ROM and press
texts.content: 5 Use the arrow keys to select CD-ROM and press .

texts.content: 6 When the Press any key to boot from CD message appears on the screen, press any key.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard drive and the speed of your computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: The Windows XP Setup screen appears, and the operating system begins to copy files and install the devices. The computer restarts multiple times.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: Completing the GUI Setup

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: NOTICE: When the computer restarts, do not press a key when the following message appears: Press any key to boot from the CD .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 1 When the Regional and Language Options screen appears, select the settings for your location and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 2 Enter your name and organization (optional) in the Personalize Your Software screen and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 3 If you are reinstalling Windows XP Home Edition , at the What's your computer's name window, enter a name for your computer (or accept the name provided) and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: If you are reinstalling Windows XP Professional , at the Computer Name and Administrator Password window, enter a name for your computer (or accept the one provided) and a password, and then click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 4 If you have a modem installed, enter the requested information and click Next when the Modem Dialing Information screen appears.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 5 Enter the date, time, and time zone in the Date and Time Settings window and click Next .

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: If your computer does not have a network adapter , this option does not appear.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: 7 If you are reinstalling Windows XP Professional, you may be prompted to provide further network information regarding your network configuration, such as the domain name or workgroup name. If you are unsure of your settings, accept the default selections.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...
texts.content: Windows XP installs the operating system components, configures the computer, and then restarts the computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...

texts.section_name: NOTE: The time required to complete the setup depends on the size of the hard...

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: Before You Add or Replace Parts

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: CAUTION: Before working inside your computer, read the safety instructions at the front of this document (see page 11).

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: 1 Ensure that the work surface is flat and clean to prevent scratching the computer cover.

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: 2 Save and close any open files, exit any open programs, and then shut down the computer (see page 40).

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: 3 Disconnect the computer from the electrical outlet.

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: 4 Wait 10 to 20 seconds, and then disconnect any attached devices.

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: 5 Remove any installed PC Cards and the battery.

texts.section_name: Before You Add or Replace Parts
texts.content: NOTICE: Handle components and cards by their edges, and avoid touching pins and contacts. Ground yourself by touching a metal connector on the back of the computer. Continue to ground yourself periodically during this procedure.

texts.section_name: Memory
texts.content: Memory

texts.section_name: Memory
texts.content: You can increase your computer memory by installing memory modules on the system board. See page 115 for information on the memory supported by your computer. Install only memory modules that are intended for your computer.

texts.section_name: Memory
texts.content: 1 Complete the instructions in the preceding section, "Before You Add or Replace Parts."

texts.section_name: Memory
texts.content: 2 Turn the computer over, loosen the captive screw on the memory module cover, and lift the cover away from the computer.

texts.section_name: Memory
texts.content: Adding and Replacing Parts 101

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer warranty.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 3 If you are replacing a memory module, remove the existing module:

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: a Use your fingertips to carefully spread apart the securing clips on each end of the memory module connector until the module pops up.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: b Remove the module from the connector.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 4 Ground yourself and install the new memory module.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: NOTE: If the memory module is not installed properly, the computer may not boot properly. No error message indicates this failure.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: a Align the notch in the module edge connector with the tab in the connector slot.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: b Slide the module firmly into the slot at a 45-degree angle, and rotate the module down until it clicks into place. If you do not feel the click, remove the module and reinstall it.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 102 Adding and Replacing Parts

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 5 Replace the cover.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: NOTICE: If the memory module cover is difficult to close, remove the module and reinstall it. Forcing the cover to close may damage your computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 6 Insert the battery into the battery bay, or connect the AC adapter to your computer and an electrical outlet.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: 7 Turn on the computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: As the computer boots, it detects the additional memory and automatically updates the system configuration information.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Memory modules purchased from Dell are covered under your computer...
texts.content: To confirm the amount of memory installed in the computer, click the Start button, click Help and Support , and then click Computer Information .

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: • To add a modem, see the following section, "Adding a Modem."

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: • To add a Mini PCI card, see page 104.

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: If you ordered the optional modem at the same time that you ordered your computer, Dell installed the modem for you.

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: 1 When you replace the modem, remove the existing modem:

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: a Remove the screws securing the modem to the system board, and set them aside.

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: b Pull straight up on the attached pull-tab to lift the modem out of its connector on the system board and disconnect the modem cable.

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: modem/Mini PCI card cover

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: 104 Adding and Replacing Parts

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: Modem and Mini PCI Card
texts.content: 2 Connect the modem cable to the modem.

texts.content: Hard Drive

texts.section_name: Hard Drive
texts.content: CAUTION: If you remove the hard drive from the computer when the drive is hot, do not touch the metal housing of the hard drive.

texts.section_name: Hard Drive
texts.content: NOTICE: To prevent data loss, shut down your computer (see page 40) before removing the hard drive. Do not remove the hard drive while the computer is on, in standby mode, or in hibernate mode.

texts.section_name: NOTICE: Hard drives are extremely fragile; even a slight bump can damage the...
texts.content: NOTICE: Hard drives are extremely fragile; even a slight bump can damage the drive.

texts.content: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives from sources other than Dell.

texts.content: Adding and Replacing Parts 107

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: To replace the hard drive in the hard drive bay:

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 1 Complete the instructions in "Before You Add or Replace Parts" on page 100.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 2 Turn the computer over, and remove the hard drive screws.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: NOTICE: When the hard drive is not in the computer, store it in protective antistatic packaging. See "Protecting Against Electrostatic Discharge" on page 17.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 3 Slide the hard drive out of the computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 4 Remove the new drive from its packaging.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: Save the original packaging for storing or shipping the hard drive.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: NOTICE: Use firm and even pressure to slide the drive into place. If you use excessive force, you may damage the connector.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 5 Insert the drive into the bay, and push the hard drive until it is fully seated in the bay.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 6 Replace and tighten the screws.

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 7 Use the Operating System CD to install the operating system for your computer (see page 94).

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...
texts.content: 8 Use the Drivers and Utilities CD to install the drivers and utilities for your computer (see page 89).

texts.section_name: NOTE: Dell does not guarantee compatibility or provide support for hard drives...

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: CD or DVD Drive

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 1 Complete the instructions in "Before You Add or Replace Parts" on page 100.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 2 Turn the computer over, remove the screw from the memory module cover, and lift the cover away from the computer.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: Adding and Replacing Parts 109

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 3 Remove the screw labeled "O" next to the memory module cover.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 4 Press the lever next to the memory module connectors in the direction of the arrow on the lever (towards the drive) to release the drive.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 5 Pull the drive out of the bay.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 6 Slide the new drive into the bay until the drive is fully seated.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 7 Replace the screw that you removed in step 3.

texts.section_name: CD or DVD Drive
texts.content: 8 Replace the memory module cover and screw.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 1 Complete the instructions in "Before You Add or Replace Parts" on page 100.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 2 Use a small flat-blade screwdriver or plastic scribe to lift the notched right edge of the hinge cover, and pry the cover loose from the hinges and bottom case.

texts.content: 110 Adding and Replacing Parts

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 3 Lift the hinge cover up and away from the hinges and bottom case.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 4 Remove the four keyboard screws.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: Adding and Replacing Parts 111

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: NOTICE: The keycaps on the keyboard are fragile, easily dislodged, and time-consuming to replace. Be careful when removing and handling the keyboard.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 5 Lift the keyboard out of the bottom case, and rest it on the display hinges so that the keyboard connector is exposed.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 6 Pull up on the keyboard connector to disconnect it from the interface connector on the system board.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 7 Remove the keyboard from the bottom case.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: NOTICE: To avoid damage to the connector pins, press the keyboard connector evenly into the interface connector on the system board, and do not reverse the keyboard connector.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 8 Connect the keyboard connector of the replacement keyboard to the interface connector on the system board.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 112 Adding and Replacing Parts

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 9 Insert the four securing tabs on the keyboard into their respective slots in the palm rest, and lower the keyboard into the bottom case.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: Ensure that all four securing tabs are engaged before trying to completely seat the keyboard.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 10 Replace the four keyboard screws.

texts.section_name: Keyboard
texts.content: 11 Replace the hinge cover.

texts.section_name: Using the System Setup Program

For more information on configuring features for your operating system, see the Help and Support Center. To access help, see page 80.

texts.section_name: Using the System Setup Program

texts.content: Viewing the System Setup Screens

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: 1 Turn on (or restart) your computer.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: 2 When the DELL™ logo appears, press immediately. If you wait too long and the Windows logo appears, continue to wait until you see the Windows desktop. Then shut down your computer (see page 40) and try again.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: System Setup Screens

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: NOTE: For information about a specific item on a system setup screen, highlight the item and see the Help area on the screen.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: On each screen, the system setup options are listed at the left. To the right of each option is the setting or value for that option. You can change settings that appear as white type on the screen. Options or values that you cannot change (because they are determined by the computer) appear less bright.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: The upper-right corner of the screen displays help information for the currently highlighted option; the lower-right corner displays information about the computer. System setup key functions are listed across the bottom of the screen.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: Certain options require that you reboot the computer for new settings to take effect.

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: Changing the Boot Sequence

texts.section_name: Storage 163 G
texts.content: The boot sequence, or boot order, tells the computer where to look to find the software needed to start the operating system. You can control the boot sequence and enable/disable devices using the Boot Order page of the system setup program.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: The Boot Order page displays a general list of the bootable devices that may be installed in your computer, including but not limited to the following:

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: • CD/DVD/CD-RW drive

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: During the boot routine, the computer starts at the top of the list and scans each enabled device for the operating system start-up files. When the computer finds the files, it stops searching and starts the operating system.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: To control the boot devices, select (highlight) a device by pressing the or key,

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: and then enable or disable the device or change its order in the list.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: • To enable or disable a device, highlight the item. Enabled items appear as white and display an arrow to the left; disabled items appear blue or dimmed without a triangle.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: • Press or to change the option.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: • To reorder a device in the list, highlight the device and then press or (not case-sensitive) to move the highlighted device up or down.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: Boot sequence changes take effect as soon as you save the changes and exit the system setup program.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: Performing a One-Time Boot

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: You can set a one-time-only boot sequence without entering the system setup program. (You can also use this procedure to boot the Dell Diagnostics on the diagnostics utility partition on your hard drive.)

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: 1 Turn off the computer.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: 2 If the computer is connected to a docking device (docked), undock it. See the documentation that came with your docking device for instructions.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: 3 Connect the computer to an electrical outlet.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: 4 Turn on the computer. When the DELL logo appears, press immediately. If you

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: wait too long and the Windows logo appears, continue to wait until you see the Windows desktop. Then shut down your computer and try again.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: 5 When the boot device list appears, highlight the device from which you want to boot

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: The computer boots to the selected device.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123
texts.content: The next time you reboot the computer, the previous boot order is restored.

texts.section_name: NOTE: To change the boot sequence on a one-time-only basis, see page 123

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: System and Battery Performance Optimization

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: System Performance Overview

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: System performance may be reduced under specific conditions to prevent automatic system shutdown and potential data loss. Conditions under which performance is reduced include:

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: PROCESSOR GETS HOT — Performance is reduced when system temperature exceeds established thermal parameters. This performance reduction helps to regulate the system and surface temperatures, which prevents the computer from shutting down due to excessive processor temperatures.

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: AC ADAPTER THROUGHPUT — Performance is reduced when the computer is running on AC power and power consumption exceeds the established parameters of the AC Adapter. This performance reduction ensures that the system does not try to consume more power than the AC adapter can provide.

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: BATTERY CAPACITY — Performance is reduced when the computer is running on battery power and power consumption exceeds the established parameters of the battery’s rated capacity or exceeds allowable safe discharge levels, or when the battery surface temperature exceeds 60°C.

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: Optimizing Power Consumption and Battery Charge Time

texts.section_name: System and Battery Performance Optimization
texts.content: The AC adapter charges a completely discharged battery in approximately 3 hours with the computer turned off. Charge time is significantly longer if the computer is on and running at high processor speeds and system activity levels. If you run your computer at high system activity levels for extended periods of time and your battery is not charging, try running your system in Low Power Mode. Low Power Mode can improve battery charge time and battery life. Depending on system activity levels, system performance may be reduced. To set Low Power Mode:

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar.

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: 1 Click the Start button → Control Panel → Performance and Maintenance → Power Options .

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: 2 Click the Power Schemes tab.

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: 3 In the Power schemes drop-down menu, click Low Power Mode .

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: For more information on power management, see the Tell Me How help file. To access the help file, see page 80.

texts.section_name: NOTE: You can also click the power meter icon on the taskbar
texts.content: The computer uses a variable speed fan to prevent the computer from overheating. The fan runs constantly, and fan speed may vary depending on usage. Fan noise is normal and does not indicate a problem with the fan or the computer.

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Technician-assisted technical support requires the cooperation and participation of the customer in the troubleshooting process and provides for restoration of the operating system, software programs, and hardware drivers to the original default configuration as shipped from Dell, as well as the verification of appropriate functionality of the computer and all Dell-installed hardware. In addition to this technician-assisted technical support, online technical support is available at support.dell.com . Additional technical support options may be available for purchase.

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Dell provides limited technical support for the computer and any "Dell-installed" software and peripherals 1 . Support for third-party software and peripherals is provided by the original manufacturer, including those purchased and/or installed through Dell Software and Peripherals, Readyware, and Custom Factory Integration 2 .

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: 1 Repair services are provided pursuant to the terms and conditions of your limited warranty and any optional support service contract purchased with the computer. 2 All Dell-standard components included in a Custom Factory Integration (CFI) project are covered by the standard Dell limited warranty for your computer. However, Dell also extends a parts replace- ment program to cover all nonstandard, third-party hardware components integrated through CFI for the duration of the computer’s service contract.

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Definition of "Dell-Installed" Software and Peripherals

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Dell-installed software includes the operating system and some of the software program that is installed on the computer during the manufacturing process (Microsoft Office, Norton Antivirus, and so on).

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Dell-installed peripherals include any internal expansion cards, or Dell-branded module bay or PC Card accessories. In addition, any Dell-branded monitors, keyboards, mice, speakers, microphones for telephonic modems, docking stations/port replicators, networking products, and all associated cabling are included.

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Definition of "Third-Party" Software and Peripherals

texts.section_name: Dell Technical Support Policy (U.S. Only)
texts.content: Third-party software and peripherals include any peripheral, accessory, or software program sold by Dell not under the Dell brand (printers, scanners, cameras, games, and so on). Support for all third-party software and peripherals is provided by the original manufacturer of the product.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: Regulatory Notices

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) is any signal or emission, radiated in free space or conducted along power or signal leads, that endangers the functioning of a radio navigation or other safety service or seriously degrades, obstructs, or repeatedly interrupts a licensed radio communications service. Radio communications services include but are not limited to AM/FM commercial broadcast, television, cellular services, radar, air-traffic control, pager, and Personal Communication Services (PCS). These licensed services, along with unintentional radiators such as digital devices, including computers, contribute to the electromagnetic environment.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC) is the ability of items of electronic equipment to function properly together in the electronic environment. While this computer has been designed and determined to be compliant with regulatory agency limits for EMI, there is no guarantee that interference will not occur in a particular installation. If this equipment does cause interference with radio communications services, which can be determined by turning the equipment off and on, you are encouraged to try to correct the interference by one or more of the following measures:

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: • Reorient the receiving antenna.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: • Relocate the computer with respect to the receiver.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: • Move the computer away from the receiver.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: • Plug the computer into a different outlet so that the computer and the receiver are on different branch circuits.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: If necessary, consult a Dell Technical Support representative or an experienced radio/television technician for additional suggestions.

texts.section_name: Regulatory Notices
texts.content: For additional regulatory information, see the Tell Me How help file that accompanied your computer. To access the help file, see page 80.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Limited Warranties and Return Policy

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell-branded hardware products purchased in the U.S. or Canada come with either a 90-day, 1-year, 2-year, 3-year, or 4-year limited warranty. To determine which warranty came with your hardware product(s), see your packing slip or invoice. The following sections describe the limited warranties and return policy for the U.S., the limited warranties and return policy for Canada, the manufacturer guarantee for Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Intel ® Pentium ® and Celeron ® warranty for the U.S. and Canada.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Limited Warranty for Dell-Branded Hardware Products (U.S. Only)

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: What is covered by this limited warranty?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: This limited warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship in your—our end-user customer's—Dell- branded hardware products, including Dell-branded peripheral products.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: What is not covered by this limited warranty?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: This limited warranty does not cover:

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Software, including the operating system and software added to the Dell-branded hardware products through our factory-integration system, third-party software, or the reloading of software

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Non-Dell-branded and Solution Provider Direct products and accessories

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Problems that result from:

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: – External causes such as accident, abuse, misuse, or problems with electrical power

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: – Servicing not authorized by Dell

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: – Usage that is not in accordance with product instructions

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: – Failure to follow the product instructions or failure to perform preventive maintenance

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: – Problems caused by using accessories, parts, or components not supplied by Dell

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Products with missing or altered Service Tags or serial numbers

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Products for which Dell has not received payment

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: THIS WARRANTY GIVES YOU SPECIFIC LEGAL RIGHTS, AND YOU MAY ALSO HAVE OTHER RIGHTS WHICH VARY FROM STATE TO STATE (OR JURISDICTION TO JURISDICTION). DELL'S RESPONSIBILITY FOR MALFUNCTIONS AND DEFECTS IN HARDWARE IS LIMITED TO REPAIR AND REPLACEMENT AS SET FORTH IN THIS WARRANTY STATEMENT. ALL EXPRESS AND IMPLIED WARRANTIES FOR THE PRODUCT, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, ARE LIMITED IN TIME TO THE TERM OF THE LIMITED WARRANTY PERIOD REFLECTED ON YOUR PACKING SLIP OR INVOICE. NO WARRANTIES, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WILL APPLY AFTER THE LIMITED WARRANTY PERIOD HAS EXPIRED. SOME STATES DO NOT ALLOW LIMITATIONS ON HOW LONG AN IMPLIED WARRANTY LASTS, SO THIS LIMITATION MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU.

OUR LIABILITY WILL BE NO MORE THAN THE AMOUNT YOU PAID FOR THE PRODUCT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF A CLAIM. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT FOR WHICH WE ARE RESPONSIBLE.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: How long does this limited warranty last?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: This limited warranty lasts for the time period indicated on your packing slip or invoice, except that the limited warranty on Dell-branded batteries lasts only 1 year and the limited warranty on the lamps for Dell-branded projectors lasts only 90 days. The limited warranty begins on the date of the packing slip or invoice. The warranty period is not extended if we repair or replace a warranted product or any parts. Dell may change the availability of limited warranties, at its discretion, but any changes will not be retroactive.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: What do I do if I need warranty service?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Before the warranty expires, please call us at the relevant number listed in the following table. Please also have your Dell Service Tag or order number available.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: During the 90 days of the 90-day limited warranty and the first year of all other limited warranties: During the 90 days of the 90-day limited warranty and the first year of all other limited warranties, we will repair any Dell- branded hardware products returned to us that prove to be defective in materials or workmanship. If we are not able to repair the product, we will replace it with a comparable product that is new or refurbished.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: When you contact us, we will issue a Return Material Authorization Number for you to include with your return. You must return the products to us in their original or equivalent packaging, prepay shipping charges, and insure the shipment or accept the risk if the product is lost or damaged in shipment. We will return the repaired or replacement products to you. We will pay to ship the repaired or replaced products to you if you use an address in the United States (excluding Puerto Rico and U.S. possessions and territories). Otherwise, we will ship the product to you freight collect.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If we determine that the product is not covered under this warranty, we will notify you and inform you of service alternatives that are available to you on a fee basis.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: NOTE: Before you ship the product(s) to us, make sure to back up the data on the hard drive(s) and any other storage device(s) in the product(s). Remove any confidential, proprietary, or personal information and removable media such as floppy disks, CDs, or PC Cards. We are not responsible for any of your confidential, proprietary, or personal information; lost or corrupted data; or damaged or lost removable media.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: During the remaining years: For the remaining period of the limited warranty, we will replace any defective part with new or refurbished parts, if we agree that it needs to be replaced. When you contact us, we will require a valid credit card number at the time you request a replacement part, but we will not charge you for the replacement part as long as you return the original part to us within 30 days after we ship the replacement part to you. If we do not receive the original part within 30 days, we will charge to your credit card the then-current standard price for that part.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: We will pay to ship the part to you if you use an address in the United States (excluding Puerto Rico and U.S. possessions and territories). Otherwise, we will ship the part freight collect. We will also include a prepaid shipping container with each replacement part for your use in returning the replaced part to us.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: NOTE: Before you replace parts, make sure to back up the data on the hard drive(s) and any other storage device(s) in the product(s). We are not responsible for lost or corrupted data.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: What if I purchased a service contract?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If your service contract is with Dell, service will be provided to you under the terms of the service agreement. Please refer to that contract for details on how to obtain service.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If you purchased through us a service contract with one of our third-party service providers, please refer to that contract for details on how to obtain service.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: How will you fix my product?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: We use new and refurbished parts made by various manufacturers in performing warranty repairs and in building replacement parts and systems. Refurbished parts and systems are parts or systems that have been returned to Dell, some of which were never used by a customer. All parts and systems are inspected and tested for quality. Replacement parts and systems are covered for the remaining period of the limited warranty for the product you bought. Dell owns all parts removed from repaired products.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: May I transfer the limited warranty?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Limited warranties on systems may be transferred if the current owner transfers ownership of the system and records the transfer with us. The limited warranty on Dell-branded memory may not be transferred. You may record your transfer by going to Dell's website:

texts.content: • If you are a Home Office, Small, Medium, Large, or Global Commercial Customer, go to www.dell.com/us/en/biz/topics/sbtopic_ccare_nav_015_ccare.htm

texts.content: "Total Satisfaction" Return Policy (U.S. Only)

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: We value our relationship with you and want to make sure that you're satisfied with your purchases. That's why we offer a "Total Satisfaction" return policy for most products that you—the end-user customer—purchase directly from Dell. Under this policy, you may return to Dell products that you purchased directly from Dell for a credit or a refund of the purchase price paid, less shipping and handling and applicable restocking fees as follows:

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • New Hardware Products and Accessories — All new hardware, accessories, parts, and unopened software still in its sealed package, excluding the products listed below, may be returned within 30 days from the date on the packing slip or invoice. To return applications software or an operating system that has been installed by Dell, you must return the entire computer. A different return policy applies to nondefective products purchased through Dell's Software and Peripherals division by customers of our Small and Medium Business divisions. Those products may be returned within 30 days from the date on the packing slip or invoice, but a fifteen percent (15%) restocking fee will be deducted from any refund or credit. The "Total Satisfaction" Return Policy and Software and Peripherals division return policy are not available for Dell | EMC storage products, EMC-branded products, or enterprise software.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • Reconditioned or Refurbished Dell-Branded Hardware Products and Parts — All reconditioned or refurbished Dell-branded server and storage products may be returned within 30 days from the date on the packing slip or invoice. All other reconditioned or refurbished Dell-branded hardware products and parts may be returned within 14 days of the date on the packing slip or invoice.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: To return products, e-mail or call Dell customer service to receive a Credit Return Authorization Number within the return policy period applicable to the product you want to return. You must obtain a Credit Return Authorization Number in order to return the product. See "Contacting Dell" on page 126 (or www.dell.com/us/en/gen/contact.htm ) to find the appropriate contact information for obtaining customer assistance.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: You must ship the products to Dell within 5 days of the date that Dell issues the Credit Return Authorization Number. You must also return the products to Dell in their original packaging, in as-new condition along with any media, documentation, and all other items that were included in the original shipment, prepay shipping charges, and insure the shipment or accept the risk of loss or damage during shipment.

texts.content: When you contact us, we will issue a Return Material Authorization Number for you to include with your return. You must return the products to us in their original or equivalent packaging, prepay shipping charges, and insure the shipment or accept the risk if the product is lost or damaged in shipment. We will return the repaired or replacement products to you. We will pay to ship the repaired or replaced products to you if you use an address in Canada. Otherwise, we will ship the product to you freight collect.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If we determine that the problem is not covered under this warranty, we will notify you and inform you of service alternatives that are available to you on a fee basis.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: NOTE: Before you ship the product(s) to us, make sure to back up the data on the hard drive(s) and any other storage device(s) in the product(s). Remove any confidential, proprietary or personal information, removable media, such as floppy disks, CDs, or PC Cards. We are not responsible for any of your confidential, proprietary or personal information; lost or corrupted data; or damaged or lost removable media.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: During the remaining years following the first year of all limited warranties: We will replace any defective part with new or refurbished parts, if we agree that it needs to be replaced. When you contact us, we will require a valid credit card number at the time you request a replacement part, but we will not charge you for the replacement part as long as you return the original part to us within 30 days after we ship the replacement part to you. If we do not receive the original part within 30 days, we will charge to your credit card the then-current standard price for that part.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: We will pay to ship the part to you if you use an address in Canada. Otherwise, we will ship the part freight collect. We will also include a prepaid shipping container with each replacement part for your use in returning the replaced part to us.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: NOTE: Before you replace parts, make sure to back up the data on the hard drive(s) and any other storage device(s) in the product(s). We are not responsible for lost or corrupted data.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: What if I purchased a service contract?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If your service contract is with Dell, service will be provided to you under the terms of the service contract. Please refer to that contract for details on how to obtain service. Dell's service contracts can be found online at www.dell.ca or by calling Customer Care at 1-800-847-4096. If you purchased through us a service contract with one of our third-party service providers, please refer to that contract (mailed to you with your packing slip or invoice) for details on how to obtain service.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: How will you fix my product?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: We use new and refurbished parts made by various manufacturers in performing warranty repairs and in building replacement parts and systems. Refurbished parts and systems are parts or systems that have been returned to Dell, some of which were never used by a customer. All parts and systems are inspected and tested for quality. Replacement parts and systems are covered for the remaining period of the limited warranty for the product you bought. Dell owns all parts removed from repaired products.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: May I transfer the limited warranty?

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Limited warranties on systems may be transferred if the current owner transfers ownership of the system and records the transfer with us. The limited warranty on Dell-branded memory may not be transferred. You may record your transfer by going to Dell’s website:

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • For Canada-purchased computers (in-country transfers) and to transfer from one customer to another, go to www.dell.ca/ca/en/gen/topics/segtopic_ccare_nav_013_ccare.htm

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: • For out-of-country transfers (outside of the original country of purchase), go to www.dell.com/us/en/biz/topics/sbtopic_ccare_nav_016_ccare.htm

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If you do not have Internet access, call Dell at 1-800-847-4096 (Home Consumer customers) or 1-800-326-9463 (Corporate Commercial or Government customers).

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: "Total Satisfaction" Return Policy (Canada Only)

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If you are an end-user customer who bought new products directly from Dell, you may return them to Dell up to 30 days after you receive them for a refund or credit of the product purchase price. If you are an end-user customer who bought reconditioned or refurbished products from Dell, you may return them to Dell within 14 days after the date on the packing slip or invoice for a refund or credit of the product purchase price. In either case, the refund or credit will not include any shipping and handling charges shown on your packing slip or invoice and will be subject to a fifteen percent (15%) restocking fee, unless otherwise prohibited by law. If you are an organization that bought the products under a written agreement with Dell, the agreement may contain different terms for the return of products than specified by this policy.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: w w w. d e l l . c o m | s u p p o r t . d e l l . c o m

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: To return products, you must call Dell Customer Service at 1-800-847-4096 to receive a Credit Return Authorization Number. To expedite the process of your refund or credit, Dell expects you to return the products to Dell in their original packaging within 5 days of the date that Dell issues the Credit Return Authorization Number. You must also prepay shipping charges and insure the shipment or accept the risk of loss or damage during shipment. You may return software for a refund or credit only if the sealed package containing the floppy disk(s) or CD(s) is unopened. Returned products must be in as-new condition, and all of the manuals, floppy disk(s), CD(s), power cables, and other items included with a product must be returned with it. For customers who want to return, for refund or credit only, either application or operating system software that has been installed by Dell, the whole system must be returned, along with any media and documentation that may have been included in the original shipment.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: The "Total Satisfaction" Return Policy does not apply to Dell | EMC storage products. It also does not apply to products purchased through Dell's Software and Peripherals division. For those products, please instead refer to Dell's Software and Peripheral's then-current return policy (see the following section, "Dell Software and Peripherals (Canada Only)").

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell Software and Peripherals (Canada Only)

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Third-Party Software and Peripherals Products

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Similar to other resellers of software and peripherals, Dell does not warrant third-party products. Third-party software and peripheral products are covered by the warranties provided by the original manufacturer or publisher only. Third-party manufacturer warranties vary from product to product. Consult your product documentation for specific warranty information. More information may also be available from the manufacturer or publisher.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: While Dell offers a wide selection of software and peripheral products, we do not specifically test or guarantee that all of the products we offer work with any or all of the various models of Dell computers, nor do we test or guarantee all of the products we sell on the hundreds of different brands of computers available today. If you have questions about compatibility, we recommend and encourage you to contact the third-party software and peripheral product manufacturer or publisher directly.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell-Branded Peripheral Products

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell does provide a limited warranty for new Dell-branded peripheral products (products for which Dell is listed as the manufacturer) such as monitors, batteries, memory, docking stations, and projectors. To determine which limited warranty applies to the product you purchased, see the Dell packing slip or invoice and/or the product documentation that accompanied your product. Descriptions of Dell's limited warranties are described in preceding sections.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: If you are an end-user customer who bought Dell Software and Peripherals products directly from a Dell company, you may return Dell Software and Peripherals products that are in as-new condition to Dell up to 30 days from the date on the packing slip or invoice for a refund of the product purchase price if already paid. This refund will not include any shipping and handling charges shown on your packing slip or invoice; you are responsible for those.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: To return products, you must call Dell Customer Service at 1-800-387-5759 to receive a Credit Return Authorization Number. You must ship the Dell Software and Peripherals products back to Dell in their original manufacturer's packaging (which must be in as-new condition), prepay shipping charges, and insure the shipment or accept the risk of loss or damage during shipment.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: To qualify for refund or replacement, returned products must be in as-new condition, software products must be unopened, and all of the manuals, floppy disk(s), CD(s), power cables, and other items included with a product must be returned with it.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: 1-Year End-User Manufacturer Guarantee (Latin America and the Caribbean Only)

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell Computer Corporation ("Dell") warrants to the end user in accordance with the following provisions that its branded hardware products, purchased by the end user from a Dell company or an authorized Dell distributor in Latin America or the Caribbean, will be free from defects in materials, workmanship, and design affecting normal use, for a period of 1 year from the original purchase date. Products for which proper claims are made will, at Dell’s option, be repaired or replaced at Dell’s expense. Dell owns all parts removed from repaired products. Dell uses new and reconditioned parts made by various manufacturers in performing repairs and building replacement products.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: This Guarantee does not apply to defects resulting from: improper or inadequate installation, use, or maintenance; actions or modifications by unauthorized third parties or the end user; accidental or willful damage; or normal wear and tear.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Claims must be made in Latin America or the Caribbean by contacting the Dell point of sale within the guarantee period. The end user must always supply proof of purchase, indicating name and address of the seller, date of purchase, model and serial number, name and address of the customer, and details of symptoms and configuration at the time of malfunction, including peripherals and software used. Otherwise, Dell may refuse the guarantee claim. Upon diagnosis of a warranted defect, Dell will make arrangements and pay for ground freight and insurance to and from Dell’s repair/replacement center. The end user must ensure that the defective product is available for collection properly packed in original or equally protective packaging together with the details listed above and the return number provided to the end user by Dell.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Limitation and Statutory Rights

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: Dell makes no other warranty, guarantee or like statement other than as explicitly stated above, and this Guarantee is given in place of all other guarantees whatsoever, to the fullest extent permitted by law. In the absence of applicable legislation, this Guarantee will be the end user’s sole and exclusive remedy against Dell or any of its affiliates, and neither Dell nor any of its affiliates shall be liable for loss of profit or contracts, or any other indirect or consequential loss arising from negligence, breach of contract, or howsoever.

texts.section_name: Limited Warranties and Return Policy
texts.content: This Guarantee does not impair or affect mandatory statutory rights of the end user against and/or any rights resulting from other contracts concluded by the end user with Dell and/or any other seller.
